description: Add Lambda function skeleton (SleepAudioProcessor) and integrate it into the Step Functions state machine as a Task state, following strict TDD with failing tests first.
steps: Create a new test file tests/unit/test_lambda_integration.py with failing tests that verify: (1) A Lambda function resource exists with Python runtime, (2) The Lambda has environment variables including the DynamoDB table name, (3) The Lambda has a handler configured, (4) The state machine definition includes a LambdaInvoke task state named 'ProcessAudio' or similar, (5) IAM permissions allow the state machine to invoke the Lambda, (6) IAM permissions allow the Lambda to read/write the DynamoDB metadata table, (7) The Lambda invocation task is positioned in the workflow at the correct spot (after WriteInitialRecord, before or after PollyTask).

Create the Lambda handler code at lambda/sleep_audio_processor/handler.py with a minimal handler that: receives input from the state machine (S3 event details, audioId), logs the input using Python logging, performs a simple action (returns enriched metadata), and includes basic error handling (try/except with structured response).

Update cdk_base/cdk_base_stack.py to: (a) Import aws_lambda, (b) Create the Lambda function using aws_lambda.Function L2 construct with Python 3.11 runtime, handler pointing to the handler module, code from asset path (lambda/sleep_audio_processor/), environment variables for DynamoDB table name, (c) Create a LambdaInvoke task state using sfn_tasks.LambdaInvoke that passes relevant input data ($.object.key and $.bucket.name), (d) Insert the Lambda invocation into the state machine chain (after WriteInitialRecord, before PollyTask), (e) Grant the Lambda DynamoDB read/write access on the metadata table using table.grant_read_write_data(lambda_fn), (f) The state machine role automatically gets lambda:InvokeFunction via the LambdaInvoke construct.
Verify all tests pass with pytest and cdk synth succeeds.

Update ARCHITECTURE.md to: (a) Change Lambda status from 'Planned' to 'Implemented' in the status table (at least the processor Lambda), (b) Update the Mermaid diagram to show the Lambda with a green fill style, (c) Update the Orchestration Layer section to show the new flow: WriteInitialRecord -> ProcessAudio (Lambda) -> PollyTask -> ..., (d) Add a section describing the Lambda's current role (placeholder processor that logs input and returns metadata) and future purpose (audio processing, validation, transcoding).
acceptance_criteria: pytest passes with all existing and new tests green
A new AWS::Lambda::Function resource appears in the synthesized template with Python 3.11 runtime
The Lambda function has environment variable for the DynamoDB table name
The state machine definition includes a LambdaInvoke task state
IAM policy allows the state machine to invoke the Lambda
IAM policy allows the Lambda to access the DynamoDB table
The Lambda handler file exists at lambda/sleep_audio_processor/handler.py with logging and error handling
ARCHITECTURE.md is updated with Lambda status, Mermaid diagram, and orchestration flow
verification: cd /projects/sandbox/cdk-sleep-py-kiro && unset NODE_OPTIONS && pyenv local 3.11.15 && pytest
cd /projects/sandbox/cdk-sleep-py-kiro && unset NODE_OPTIONS && pyenv local 3.11.15 && cdk synth > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo 'synth OK'
